article.title: Central bank holds benchmark rate, points to easing inflation
article.text: The central bank left its main interest rate unchanged on Tuesday, signalling confidence that inflation continues to slow toward its policy target. The decision, widely anticipated by economists, marks a fourth straight month of declining price growth. Officials noted that the labour market remains resilient and reiterated their commitment to a data-dependent path. Energy prices were flagged as the primary upside risk. Bond yields edged lower after the announcement, while the currency was little changed against the dollar.
